🌱 Indian Crop Calendar:

☀️ KHARIF (Jun–Nov): Rice, Maize, Cotton, Soybean, Groundnut, Sugarcane, Jowar, Bajra, Turmeric

❄️ RABI (Nov–Apr): Wheat, Mustard, Onion, Garlic, Gram, Peas, Barley, Coriander

🌤️ ZAID (Apr–Jun): Watermelon, Cucumber, Muskmelon, Pumpkin, Moong, Sunflower

💡 Which specific crop do you want to know about? Type the crop name!

🌾 CROP SELECTION GUIDE by region:

📍 Maharashtra: Onion, Sugarcane, Soybean, Cotton, Grape, Pomegranate
📍 Punjab/Haryana: Wheat, Rice, Maize, Mustard, Potato
📍 UP/Bihar: Wheat, Sugarcane, Rice, Potato, Vegetable crops
📍 Rajasthan: Bajra, Jowar, Mustard, Groundnut, Moth Bean
📍 Karnataka/AP: Rice, Jowar, Sunflower, Groundnut, Chilli

Ask about any specific crop for a complete guide!

🌿 CROP ROTATION BENEFITS:

Proper crop rotation prevents soil exhaustion and breaks pest cycles!

✅ Cereal → Legume → Vegetable (Best rotation)
✅ Rice → Wheat → Legume (North India popular)
✅ Cotton → Wheat → Groundnut (Central India)
✅ Onion → Maize → Onion (Nashik region)

🔑 Always include a nitrogen-fixing crop (Gram, Soybean, Moong) in rotation — saves 30–40 kg N fertilizer cost!

Which crop do you want detailed info on?

📊 TOP PROFITABLE CROPS 2024-25:

1️⃣ Onion — ₹1,500–4,000/quintal (high demand)
2️⃣ Tomato — ₹1,000–5,000/quintal (volatile)
3️⃣ Grape (Export) — ₹40–120/kg
4️⃣ Pomegranate — ₹50–150/kg
5️⃣ Garlic — ₹3,000–8,000/quintal
6️⃣ Capsicum (Polyhouse) — ₹20–60/kg
7️⃣ Baby Corn — ₹15–30/kg

⚠️ Profitability depends on season, location & market. Always check local mandi rates!

🌱 SEED QUALITY GUIDE:

Always use certified seeds for best results:

✅ BT SEEDS: Buy only from authorized dealers
✅ HYBRID SEEDS: Higher yield but can't save seeds
✅ OPEN POLLINATED (OP): Can save seeds for next season
✅ TREATED SEEDS: Pre-treated with fungicide/insecticide

📍 Where to buy quality seeds:
• Government seed stores (Krishi Bhavan)
• NSC (National Seeds Corporation)
• State seeds corporations
• Registered private dealers

⚠️ Always check expiry date & germination % on packet!

🌾 WHEAT FARMING — Complete Guide:

📅 Season: Rabi | Sow: Oct 15 – Nov 25
🌡️ Temperature: 10–25°C (germination 20–25°C)
🌧️ Rainfall: 30–100 cm

🌱 TOP VARIETIES:
• HD-2967 (Popular, 145 days)
• PBW-343 (Punjab, 155 days)
• GW-322 (Gujarat)
• WR-544 (Disease resistant)
• DBW-187 (High yielding, 102–107 days)

📐 Spacing: Row-to-row 20–22 cm
🌱 Seed rate: 100 kg/ha (timely) / 125 kg/ha (late)

💊 Fertilizer: 120:60:40 kg/ha (N:P:K)
→ Half N + full P + full K at sowing
→ Remaining N at first irrigation (CRI stage)

🌊 Irrigations: 5–6 times
1st: Crown Root Initiation (20–25 days)
2nd: Tillering (40–45 days)
3rd: Jointing (60–65 days)

📈 Yield: 40–50 quintal/ha
💰 MSP 2024-25: ₹2,275/quintal

🌾 WHEAT — Pest & Disease Guide:

🦠 DISEASES:
1. Yellow Rust (Puccinia striiformis)
→ Yellow stripes on leaves
→ Spray: Propiconazole 25 EC @ 0.1%

2. Brown Rust (P. recondita)
→ Brown pustules on leaves
→ Spray: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 g/L

3. Loose Smut (Ustilago tritici)
→ Earheads turn black/smutty
→ Treat seeds with Carboxin @ 2.5 g/kg

4. Powdery Mildew
→ White powder on leaves
→ Spray: Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 1 ml/L

🪲 PESTS:
• Aphids: Dimethoate 30 EC @ 1.5 ml/L
• Termite: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC soil treatment
• Weevil (stored wheat): Malathion dust

💡 Tip: Spray fungicide at flag leaf stage for maximum protection!

🌾 WHEAT — Advanced Tips for High Yield:

🎯 TARGET: 50+ quintal/ha

1️⃣ TIMELY SOWING: Oct 25 – Nov 10 gives max yield
→ Late sowing (after Nov 25) = 1.5% yield loss per day!

2️⃣ SEED TREATMENT (must do):
→ Vitavax Power @ 2.5 g/kg seed
→ Trichoderma @ 4 g/kg seed
→ PSB + Azospirillum bio-fertilizer

3️⃣ BORON APPLICATION:
→ Borax 0.5% spray at ear emergence
→ Increases grain weight by 8–12%

4️⃣ ZINC SULPHATE:
→ 25 kg/ha if deficiency symptoms seen
→ Yellowing between leaf veins = zinc deficiency

5️⃣ WEED CONTROL:
→ 2,4-D @ 500 g/ha at 30–35 days
→ Isoproturon for grassy weeds

📈 Extra income: Straw = ₹150–300/quintal (sell to dairy)

🌾 WHEAT MARKET & STORAGE:

💰 PRICES 2024-25:
• MSP (Government): ₹2,275/quintal
• Open market: ₹2,350–2,600/quintal
• Flour mills (direct): ₹2,400–2,650/quintal

🏪 STORAGE TIPS:
→ Dry to <12% moisture before storing
→ Use PUSA bin or metal bins
→ Hermetic bags (PICS/ZeroFly) for small lots
→ Malathion 5% dust: 50g/quintal
→ Aluminum Phosphide (Celphos) tablet: 1 tablet/tonne

⚠️ DON'T: Store in jute bags without treatment

📈 SELLING STRATEGY:
• Sell 40% immediately at harvest
• Store 60% — sell in Aug–Sep when prices peak
• Average price diff: ₹300–500/quintal

🏛️ Pledge Financing: Get 75% of MSP as loan from warehouse!

🧅 ONION FARMING — Complete Guide:

📅 SEASONS:
• Kharif: Sow Jun–Jul, Harvest Oct–Nov
• Late Kharif: Sow Aug–Sep, Harvest Dec–Jan
• Rabi: Sow Oct–Nov, Harvest Mar–May (BEST)

🌱 TOP VARIETIES:
• Nasik Red (most popular)
• Agrifound Light Red (long shelf life)
• Bhima Red (Kharif)
• Bhima Shakti, N-53, Phule Samarth

🌡️ Temperature: 13–24°C (bulb formation)
🧪 Soil pH: 6.0–7.5
📐 Spacing: 15 × 10 cm

💊 FERTILIZER:
Basal: 50 kg N + 50 kg P + 50 kg K/ha
Top dress: 50 kg N at 30 days after transplanting
+ 10 kg Sulphur (for pungency & shelf life)

🌊 IRRIGATION: Every 7–10 days via drip/sprinkler
⚠️ Stop irrigation 10–15 days before harvest!

📈 YIELD: 250–350 quintal/ha
💰 Nashik Mandi: ₹1,200–3,000/quintal

🧅 ONION — Diseases & Pests:

🦠 MAJOR DISEASES:

1. PURPLE BLOTCH (Most common!)
→ Symptoms: Purple spots with yellow halo on leaves
→ Treatment: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2g/L every 7 days
→ Also: Iprodione 50 WP @ 1.5 g/L

2. STEMPHYLIUM BLIGHT
→ Symptoms: Yellow-orange spots, leaf tip dieback
→ Treatment: Tebuconazole 25.9 EC @ 0.5 ml/L

3. BASAL ROT (Fusarium)
→ Symptoms: Yellow leaves, rotting from base
→ Prevention: Trichoderma seed treatment
→ Soil drench: Carbendazim @ 1 g/L

4. DOWNY MILDEW
→ Pale green/yellow patches, violet mold
→ Treatment: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2.5 g/L

🪲 PESTS:
• THRIPS (most damaging):
→ Spinosad 45 SC @ 0.3 ml/L
→ Fipronil 5 SC @ 1.5 ml/L

• ONION FLY MAGGOT:
→ Chlorpyriphos 20 EC soil treatment before transplanting

🧅 ONION MARKET & STORAGE — Expert Guide:

📈 PRICE TREND (Nashik/Lasalgaon):
• Mar–May (harvest): ₹800–1,500/quintal (low)
• Jun–Aug (lean season): ₹2,000–4,000/quintal
• Oct–Dec (Kharif arrival): ₹600–1,200/quintal

💡 BEST STRATEGY: Store Rabi crop → sell in Jun–Aug!

🏪 STORAGE METHODS:

1. TRADITIONAL CHHAJALI (Bamboo):
→ Ventilated structure, 65–70% humidity
→ Loss: 15–20% over 6 months
→ Cost: ₹5,000–8,000/acre storage

2. ONION STORAGE SHED (Recommended):
→ Wire mesh walls for air circulation
→ North-South orientation
→ Loss reduced to 8–12%
→ Subsidy available under RKVY/NHM

3. COLD STORAGE:
→ Temperature: 0–2°C, 65–70% RH
→ Duration: 8–10 months
→ Cost: ₹200–300/quintal

📱 LIVE RATES:
• Lasalgaon APMC: lasalgaon.com
• Agmarknet: agmarknet.nic.in

🧅 ONION — Advanced Cultivation Tips:

🎯 FOR 300+ QUINTAL/HA YIELD:

1️⃣ NURSERY MANAGEMENT:
→ Raised bed nursery: 1m × 3m beds
→ Sow 10 kg seed/ha (6–7 g/sq.m)
→ Soil drench with Bavistin before sowing
→ Seedling age: 7–8 weeks (45–50 cm height)

2️⃣ TRANSPLANTING TIPS:
→ Transplant in evening or cloudy weather
→ Dip roots in Carbendazim + Imidacloprid solution
→ Maintain soil moisture for 5–7 days after transplanting

3️⃣ BULBING STAGE CARE:
→ Apply Potash (MOP) @ 50 kg/ha at 45 days
→ Sulphur spray 0.2%: improves color & pungency
→ Reduce irrigation frequency at bulbing

4️⃣ GRADING FOR BETTER PRICE:
→ Grade A (65mm+): Export quality, ₹500 premium
→ Grade B (45–65mm): Premium domestic
→ Grade C (below 45mm): Processing/domestic

🏛️ EXPORT OPPORTUNITIES:
→ Malaysia, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh are top buyers
→ Register with APEDA for export documentation

🧅 ONION — Organic & IPM Approach:

🌿 ORGANIC ONION FARMING:

💊 FERTILIZER (Organic):
→ FYM: 20 tonnes/ha (well decomposed)
→ Vermicompost: 5 tonnes/ha
→ Neem cake: 250 kg/ha
→ Bio-fertilizer: Azospirillum + PSB

🌱 FOLIAR NUTRITION:
→ Panchagavya 3%: every 15 days
→ Jeevamrit: 200L/acre/month
→ Fish amino acid 3%: at bulbing stage

🪲 IPM (Integrated Pest Management):
→ Yellow sticky traps: 10/acre (for Thrips)
→ Blue sticky traps for Thrips monitoring
→ Install bird perches (T-shaped stakes)
→ NSKE (Neem Seed Kernel Extract) 5%: spray
→ Beauveria bassiana @ 5 g/L for Thrips

💰 ORGANIC PREMIUM:
→ 15–30% higher price
→ Direct sell to organic stores & online
→ Certification: PGS-India (2 years vs 3 for NPOP)

🍅 TOMATO FARMING — Complete Guide:

📅 SEASONS:
• Jun–Jul (Kharif): Harvest Sep–Nov
• Oct–Nov (Rabi): Harvest Jan–Mar (Best)
• Jan–Feb (Summer): Harvest Apr–May

🌱 TOP VARIETIES:
• Arka Rakshak (hybrid, disease resistant)
• Pusa Ruby (open pollinated)
• Solan Vajra (hilly areas)
• Namdhari NS-501 (commercial hybrid)
• Lakshmi (processing type)

📐 Spacing: 60 × 45 cm | Seed: 200–250 g/ha
💊 FERTILIZER: 150:75:75 kg/ha (N:P:K)
→ Split N in 3 doses: Basal, 30 days, 60 days
→ Add Calcium Nitrate @ 10 g/L to prevent BER

🌊 IRRIGATION: Drip preferred (3–4 days interval)
→ Critical stages: Flowering & fruit development

⚒️ STAKING: Essential — prevents fruit touching soil

📈 YIELD: 250–400 quintal/ha
💰 Price: ₹500–5,000/quintal (highly seasonal)

🍅 TOMATO — Diseases & Treatment:

🦠 TOP DISEASES:

1. EARLY BLIGHT (Alternaria solani) ⭐
→ Dark brown spots with yellow halo & rings
→ Spray: Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 g/L
→ Or: Iprodione 50 WP @ 1.5 g/L

2. LATE BLIGHT (Phytophthora) 🚨 CRITICAL
→ Water-soaked lesions, white mold below leaf
→ Spreads FAST in cool wet weather!
→ Spray IMMEDIATELY: Metalaxyl+Mancozeb @ 2.5 g/L

3. FUSARIUM WILT
→ One side yellowing, brown vascular tissue
→ Prevention: Trichoderma soil treatment
→ Soil drench: Carbendazim 1 g/L

4. BACTERIAL WILT (Ralstonia)
→ Sudden wilting, slimy stem ooze
→ No chemical cure — remove plants
→ Prevention: Crop rotation, raised beds

5. TOMATO YELLOW LEAF CURL VIRUS (TYLCV)
→ Curled yellow leaves, stunted plant
→ Manage whitefly vector
→ Imidacloprid @ 0.3 ml/L

6. BLOSSOM END ROT (BER)
→ Black sunken area at fruit bottom
→ Calcium deficiency + irregular irrigation
→ Spray: Calcium Nitrate 10 g/L

🍅 TOMATO — Pest Management:

🪲 MAJOR PESTS:

1. FRUIT BORER (Helicoverpa) 🚨 #1 Problem!
→ Holes in fruits, frass near entry point
→ ORGANIC: Bacillus thuringiensis 1 g/L
→ Chemical: Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 0.3 ml/L
→ Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 0.4 g/L
→ Pheromone traps: 5/acre

2. WHITEFLY
→ Sticky honeydew, TYLCV virus vector
→ Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 0.3 ml/L
→ Thiamethoxam 25 WG @ 0.3 g/L
→ Yellow sticky traps: 10/acre

3. APHIDS
→ Curling leaves, honeydew secretion
→ Neem oil 5 ml/L or Dimethoate 1.5 ml/L

4. MITES (Tetranychus)
→ Bronze-colored leaves, fine webbing
→ Abamectin 1.8 EC @ 0.5 ml/L
→ Spray on undersides of leaves!

5. LEAF MINER
→ Serpentine mines on leaves
→ Spinosad 45 SC @ 0.3 ml/L

💡 Tip: Rotate insecticides to prevent resistance!

🍅 TOMATO MARKET & SELLING STRATEGY:

📈 PRICE CALENDAR:
• Jun–Aug: High prices (₹2,000–5,000/quintal)
• Oct–Dec: Moderate (₹1,000–2,500)
• Jan–Mar: Low (harvest season, ₹300–800)

💡 MAXIMIZE PROFITS:

1️⃣ POLY HOUSE GROWING:
→ Control season, get ₹3,000–8,000/quintal in off-season
→ Cost: ₹8–12 lakh/acre (25–50% subsidy available)

2️⃣ GRADING & PACKAGING:
→ Grade A: 60g+ fruits → Export/Modern retail
→ Premium pack in 10-kg corrugated boxes
→ Price premium: 20–40%

3️⃣ DIRECT MARKETING:
→ Sell directly to sabzi mandis, hotels, restaurants
→ Register on e-NAM for national market
→ Join FPO for collective bargaining

4️⃣ VALUE ADDITION:
→ Tomato paste/ketchup: ₹50–80/kg (value added)
→ Sun-dried tomatoes (export): ₹300–500/kg

📱 Price apps: Commodity India, Agrimart, e-NAM

🌿 COTTON FARMING — Complete Guide:

📅 Season: Kharif | Sow: May–Jun
🌡️ Temperature: 25–35°C | Rain: 50–100 cm
🧪 Soil: Deep black cotton soil (Vertisol) — BEST!

🌱 VARIETIES:
• Bt Cotton (recommended): MRC-7017, NCS-145, RCH-2, MRC-6304
• Desi/Non-Bt: Suvin, Jayadhar (for organic farming)

📐 Spacing: 90 × 60 cm (irrigated) / 120 × 60 cm (rainfed)
🌱 Seed rate: 1 kg/acre

💊 FERTILIZER:
→ Basal: 30N + 60P + 60K kg/ha
→ 1st top dress (45 days): 30 kg N
→ 2nd top dress (90 days): 30 kg N + 30 kg K
→ Micronutrients: Zinc 25 kg/ha + Boron 1 kg/ha

🌊 IRRIGATION: 5–7 times
→ Critical at square formation & boll filling stages

📈 YIELD: 15–30 quintal/ha (seed cotton)
💰 MSP 2024-25: ₹7,121/quintal (long staple)

🌿 COTTON — Pest Management (Pink Bollworm Crisis!):

🚨 PINK BOLLWORM (PBW) — Most Critical Pest
→ Larvae enter bolls, damage fiber
→ Entry point: Small hole with frass
→ CONTROL:
  • Pheromone traps: 10/ha (monitoring)
  • Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC: 0.3 ml/L
  • Profenophos 50 EC: 2 ml/L
  • Cyfluthrin spray at 50 days interval

🪲 OTHER PESTS:
1. WHITEFLY (Bemisia tabaci):
→ Yellowing, leaf curl virus vector
→ Diafenthiuron 50 WP: 1.5 g/L
→ Flonicamid 50 WG: 0.3 g/L

2. MEALYBUG:
→ White cottony mass on stems
→ Profenophos + Cypermethrin: 2 ml/L
→ Release Cryptolaemus beetles

3. THRIPS:
→ Silver streaking on leaves
→ Spinosad 45 SC: 0.3 ml/L

4. APHIDS:
→ Dimethoate 30 EC: 1.5 ml/L

💡 Follow HTBT cotton management guidelines strictly!

🌿 COTTON — Disease & Weed Control:

🦠 DISEASES:

1. BACTERIAL BLIGHT (Black arm)
→ Water-soaked angular lesions on leaves
→ Treatment: Streptomycin + Copper Oxychloride
→ Prevention: Use resistant varieties

2. ALTERNARIA LEAF SPOT
→ Brown circular spots with yellow halo
→ Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 g/L

3. FUSARIUM WILT
→ Yellowing, wilting, brown stem inside
→ Trichoderma soil treatment
→ Carbendazim seed treatment @ 2 g/kg

4. GREY MILDEW
→ White/grey powdery growth on leaves
→ Karathane (Dinocap) @ 1 ml/L

🌿 WEED MANAGEMENT:
→ Pre-emergence: Pendimethalin 30 EC @ 3 L/ha
→ Post-emergence: Quizalofop Ethyl for grassy weeds
→ 2 hand weedings: 15 & 35 days after sowing

💡 Tip: Intercrop with Soybean or Moong to maximize income + suppress weeds!

🌾 RICE/PADDY FARMING — Complete Guide:

📅 Season: Kharif | Transplant: Jun–Jul
🌡️ Temp: 25–35°C | Rain: 100–200 cm
🧪 Soil: Clay loam, pH 5.5–7.0

🌱 VARIETIES:
• IR-64 (semi-dwarf, high yield)
• Swarna/MTU-7029 (flood tolerant)
• Pusa Basmati 1121 (premium export)
• HMT Sona, Samba Mahsuri
• DRR Dhan 44 (drought tolerant)

📐 SRI METHOD (Recommended):
→ Young seedlings: 8–12 days
→ Single seedling/hill, 25×25 cm spacing
→ Alternate wetting & drying (AWD)
→ Saves 30% water, increases yield 20–30%!

💊 FERTILIZER: 120:60:60 kg/ha (N:P:K)
→ Zinc sulphate: 25 kg/ha (very important!)
→ Split N: 50% basal + 25% tillering + 25% panicle

📈 YIELD: 40–60 quintal/ha
💰 MSP 2024-25: ₹2,300/quintal

🌾 RICE — Diseases & Pests:

🦠 DISEASES:

1. BLAST (Pyricularia oryzae) — Most Dangerous!
→ Diamond-shaped lesions on leaves (neck/leaf blast)
→ IMMEDIATE treatment: Tricyclazole 75 WP @ 0.6 g/L
→ Tebuconazole 25.9 EC @ 0.5 ml/L
→ Resistant varieties: Swarna Sub1, Pusa Basmati

2. BROWN SPOT (Helminthosporium)
→ Oval brown spots with yellow halo
→ Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 g/L
→ Caused by poor nutrition (K & Mn deficiency)

3. BACTERIAL LEAF BLIGHT (BLB)
→ Water-soaked, wavy yellow lesions from leaf tips
→ No effective chemical — use resistant varieties
→ Reduce N, drain water during infection

4. SHEATH BLIGHT (Rhizoctonia)
→ Oval lesions on sheath, plants lodge
→ Hexaconazole 5 EC @ 1 ml/L
→ Validamycin A 3L @ 2 ml/L

🪲 PESTS:
• Stem Borer: Dead heart, white ear — Chlorpyriphos granules
• Brown Planthopper: Buprofezin 25 SC @ 1.5 ml/L
• Leaf Folder: Chlorantraniliprole @ 0.3 ml/L
• Gall Midge: Carbofuran 3G @ 25 kg/ha

🪲 PEST IDENTIFICATION & CONTROL:

🔍 SUCKING PESTS (pierce & suck plant sap):
• Aphids, Whitefly, Thrips, Mealybug, Scale insects
→ ORGANIC: Neem oil 5 ml/L + soap 5 g/L
→ CHEMICAL: Imidacloprid 17.8 SL @ 0.3 ml/L
→ Or: Thiamethoxam 25 WG @ 0.3 g/L
→ Or: Flonicamid 50 WG @ 0.3 g/L

🐛 CHEWING PESTS (eat plant tissue):
• Caterpillars, Beetles, Cutworms, Armyworms
→ ORGANIC: Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis) 1 g/L
→ CHEMICAL: Chlorpyriphos 20 EC @ 2 ml/L
→ Or: Emamectin Benzoate 5 SG @ 0.4 g/L
→ Or: Spinosad 45 SC @ 0.3 ml/L

🕷️ MITES:
• Spider mite, Red mite, Yellow mite
→ Abamectin 1.8 EC @ 0.5 ml/L
→ Hexythiazox 5.45 EC @ 1 ml/L
→ Spray on leaf undersides!

🦟 SOIL PESTS:
• Termite, Grubs, Cut worms
→ Chlorpyriphos 20 EC: soil drench
→ Phorate 10G: 25 kg/ha

⚠️ SAFETY: Wear PPE while spraying! Wait for PHI!

🪲 INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM):

🎯 IPM = Right Pest + Right Method + Right Time

1️⃣ CULTURAL METHODS (First line of defense):
→ Deep summer plowing (kills soil pests & eggs)
→ Crop rotation (breaks pest cycles)
→ Resistant varieties
→ Proper plant spacing (good air circulation)
→ Remove and destroy infected plants

2️⃣ MECHANICAL METHODS:
→ Yellow sticky traps: Whitefly, Aphids, Leaf miners
→ Blue sticky traps: Thrips monitoring
→ Pheromone traps: Bollworm, Fruit fly, FAW
→ Light traps: Nocturnal moths
→ Install bird perches (T-stakes) for natural predators

3️⃣ BIOLOGICAL CONTROL:
→ Trichogramma cards (egg parasitoid): 5 cards/acre
→ Chrysoperla (lacewing larvae): Aphids
→ NPV (Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus): Caterpillars
→ Pseudomonas fluorescens: Soil pathogens
→ Trichoderma: Root diseases

4️⃣ CHEMICAL (LAST resort):
→ Use only when economic threshold is crossed
→ Rotate chemicals to prevent resistance
→ Spot spray, don't spray entire field

💡 80% pest problems can be solved without chemicals!

🪲 ORGANIC PEST CONTROL RECIPES:

🌿 HOME-MADE SPRAYS (Cost-effective!):

1️⃣ NEEM SPRAY:
→ Crush 2 kg neem leaves in 10L water overnight
→ Filter & spray (controls most soft-bodied insects)
→ Or: Neem oil 5 ml + soap 3 g per liter

2️⃣ GARLIC-CHILLI SPRAY:
→ 100g garlic + 100g green chilli
→ Grind, add to 10L water, strain & spray
→ Repels aphids, mites, caterpillars

3️⃣ SOAP SPRAY:
→ 5g liquid soap per liter water
→ Spray on aphids, whitefly, mites
→ Don't use on tender seedlings!

4️⃣ PANCHGAVYA SPRAY:
→ 30 ml panchgavya per liter water
→ Boosts plant immunity, repels insects
→ Apply every 10–15 days

5️⃣ TOBACCO DECOCTION:
→ Boil 100g tobacco leaves in 2L water
→ Cool, filter, dilute to 10L, add soap
→ Controls sucking pests effectively

🔑 Spray in evening or early morning for best results!

🪲 PESTICIDE SAFETY & RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT:

⚠️ BEFORE SPRAYING — MUST READ:

🛡️ PERSONAL PROTECTION:
→ Wear: Gloves, face mask, eye protection
→ Full sleeve clothes while spraying
→ Don't eat/drink/smoke while spraying
→ Wash hands & face after spraying
→ Store chemicals away from food & children

⏰ PRE-HARVEST INTERVAL (PHI) — Don't harvest before:
→ Organophosphates: 7–14 days
→ Pyrethroids: 7 days
→ Neonicotinoids: 7–14 days
→ Fungicides: 3–7 days

🔄 RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT:
→ Never use same chemical more than 2–3 times/season
→ Rotate between chemical groups (IRAC groups)
→ Use tank mixes (2 different MOA chemicals)

📊 CHEMICAL GROUPS (rotate these):
→ Group 1A: Organophosphates (Chlorpyriphos)
→ Group 4A: Neonicotinoids (Imidacloprid)
→ Group 5: Spinosyns (Spinosad)
→ Group 28: Diamides (Chlorantraniliprole)

🌿 PLANT DISEASE DIAGNOSIS GUIDE:

📋 DESCRIBE YOUR PROBLEM:

🍂 LEAF SYMPTOMS:
→ Yellow leaves = Nitrogen deficiency OR virus
→ Brown spots with rings = Early Blight
→ White powder = Powdery Mildew
→ Purple/dark spots = Purple Blotch, Anthracnose
→ Water-soaked lesions = Late Blight, Bacterial
→ Yellow mosaic pattern = Viral disease

🌱 STEM/ROOT SYMPTOMS:
→ Wilting suddenly = Fusarium/Bacterial Wilt
→ Rotting stem base = Collar Rot, Root Rot
→ Black/brown stem = Damping off, Stem rot

🍅 FRUIT SYMPTOMS:
→ Rotting from top = Anthracnose
→ Black sunken bottom = BER (Blossom End Rot)
→ Cracking = Irregular irrigation
→ Sunken spots = Anthracnose

💊 GENERAL FUNGICIDE SCHEDULE:
→ Mancozeb 75 WP @ 2.5 g/L (preventive)
→ Carbendazim 50 WP @ 1 g/L (curative)
→ Spray every 7–10 days during humid weather

🌿 FUNGAL DISEASES — Master Treatment Guide:

🦠 TOP FUNGICIDES & USES:

📦 CONTACT FUNGICIDES (Preventive):
• Mancozeb 75 WP — Blights, Downy mildew, Rust
• Copper Oxychloride 50 WP — Bacterial + Fungal
• Chlorothalonil 75 WP — Early Blight, Botrytis
• Zineb 75 WP — General fungal diseases

📦 SYSTEMIC FUNGICIDES (Curative):
• Carbendazim 50 WP — Wilt, Powdery mildew
• Propiconazole 25 EC — Rust, Powdery mildew
• Hexaconazole 5 EC — Powdery mildew, Scab
• Tebuconazole 25.9 EC — Rust, Blotch, Scab
• Metalaxyl + Mancozeb — Downy mildew, Late blight
• Azoxystrobin 23 SC — Blast, Anthracnose

💡 COMBINATION STRATEGY:
→ Preventive spray (Mancozeb) at first sign
→ If infection starts: Switch to systemic
→ Alternate contact + systemic to prevent resistance
→ Add spreader-sticker (0.5 ml/L) for better coverage

⚠️ Never spray same fungicide more than 3 times/season!

🌿 VIRAL DISEASES — Identification & Management:

🦠 COMMON PLANT VIRUSES IN INDIA:

1. TOMATO YELLOW LEAF CURL VIRUS (TYLCV)
→ Crops: Tomato, Chilli, Pepper
→ Vector: Whitefly (Bemisia tabaci)
→ Symptoms: Upward leaf curl, yellow margins, stunting
→ Management:
  • Control whitefly with Imidacloprid @ 0.3 ml/L
  • Yellow sticky traps 10/acre
  • Use silver reflective mulch
  • Remove infected plants early

2. CUCUMBER MOSAIC VIRUS (CMV)
→ Crops: Cucurbits, Tomato, Chilli, Groundnut
→ Vector: Aphids
→ Symptoms: Mosaic pattern, leaf distortion
→ Management: Control aphids

3. YELLOW MOSAIC VIRUS (YMV)
→ Crops: Soybean, Moong, Urad
→ Vector: Whitefly
→ Symptoms: Bright yellow-green mosaic on leaves
→ Use resistant varieties (MACS-330 for soybean)

4. GROUNDNUT BUD NECROSIS VIRUS (GBNV)
→ Crops: Groundnut, Tomato, Capsicum
→ Vector: Thrips
→ Symptoms: Stunting, bud necrosis, ring spots
→ Control Thrips with Spinosad @ 0.3 ml/L

⚠️ KEY RULE: No chemical cure for viruses — PREVENT spread by controlling vectors!

🌿 SOIL-BORNE DISEASES — Prevention & Cure:

🔬 MAJOR SOIL PATHOGENS:

1. FUSARIUM WILT (Fusarium oxysporum)
→ Crops: Tomato, Cotton, Banana, Gram, Watermelon
→ Symptoms: Yellowing one side, brown vascular tissue
→ Prevention: Trichoderma viride @ 4 g/kg seed
→ Soil application: Trichoderma 2.5 kg + FYM/acre

2. PYTHIUM ROOT ROT
→ Crops: Seedlings of all crops (Damping off)
→ Symptoms: Collar rot, seedling collapse
→ Treatment: Metalaxyl seed treatment
→ Soil drench: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2.5 g/L

3. SCLEROTIUM STEM ROT
→ Crops: Groundnut, Soybean, Sunflower, Vegetables
→ White cottony mycelium at base
→ Treatment: Pencycuron 22 SC @ 1.5 ml/L

4. PHYTOPHTHORA ROOT ROT
→ Crops: Avocado, Soybean, Black Pepper
→ Metalaxyl + Mancozeb soil drench

🌿 BIOLOGICAL CONTROL (Best for soil diseases):
→ Trichoderma viride/harzianum: 2.5 kg/acre
→ Pseudomonas fluorescens: 2.5 kg/acre
→ Mix with 200 kg compost, broadcast in field
→ Also effective: Bacillus subtilis

💡 SOIL SOLARIZATION:
→ Cover moist soil with transparent plastic
→ 4–6 weeks in summer (temp reaches 45–50°C)
→ Kills pathogens, weed seeds & nematodes!

💊 FERTILIZER GUIDE — NPK & Micronutrients:

🔢 PRIMARY NUTRIENTS:
🌿 N (NITROGEN) — Growth & green color
→ Deficiency: Yellow older leaves, stunted growth
→ Sources: Urea (46%), CAN (26%), Ammonium Sulphate (21%)

🌸 P (PHOSPHORUS) — Roots, flowers, seeds
→ Deficiency: Purple/red leaves, poor root growth
→ Sources: DAP (18:46), SSP (16% P), TSP (46% P)

🍎 K (POTASSIUM) — Fruit quality, disease resistance
→ Deficiency: Brown leaf edges, poor fruit quality
→ Sources: MOP (60%), SOP (50%), NPK complex

🧬 SECONDARY & MICRONUTRIENTS:
→ Zinc (ZnSO4 21%): 25 kg/ha — very common deficiency!
→ Boron (Borax): 10 kg/ha — critical for fruit crops
→ Calcium (CaNO3): BER prevention in tomato
→ Sulphur (Gypsum/Bentonite S): 20–25 kg/ha
→ Magnesium (MgSO4): Foliar 10 g/L for deficiency

🌿 ORGANIC SOIL MANAGEMENT:

♻️ ORGANIC MATTER SOURCES:

1️⃣ FYM (Farm Yard Manure):
→ Nutrient: 0.5% N + 0.25% P + 0.5% K
→ Rate: 10–15 tonnes/ha
→ Must be well-decomposed (4–6 months old)
→ Apply 1 month before sowing

2️⃣ VERMICOMPOST:

→ Grow & incorporate at 45–50 days (pre-flowering)

4️⃣ CROP RESIDUE MANAGEMENT:
→ Don't burn stubble! Burns nutrients!
→ Chop & incorporate with rotavator
→ Speeds decomposition: Trichocompost

5️⃣ BIOFERTILIZERS:
→ Rhizobium: Legume seed treatment (fixes 50–80 kg N!)
→ Azospirillum: Cereals & grasses
→ PSB (Phosphate Solubilizing Bacteria): All crops
→ Azotobacter: Vegetables & fruits

💡 Organic carbon >0.75% = Healthy soil!

🧪 SOIL TESTING — How to & Interpretation:

📋 WHEN TO TEST SOIL:
→ Every 2–3 years OR before new crop season
→ After flood/drought to assess damage
→ If crop shows deficiency symptoms

📦 SOIL SAMPLE COLLECTION:
1. Collect from 8–10 spots in field
2. Depth: 0–15 cm (surface) & 15–30 cm
3. Mix all samples → take 500g composite
4. Air dry, remove stones & roots
5. Fill in sample bag with field details

📊 SOIL TEST RESULTS — INTERPRETATION:

🟢 GOOD LEVELS:
→ pH: 6.5–7.5
→ EC: <0.8 dS/m
→ Organic Carbon: >0.75%
→ N: >280 kg/ha
→ P: >25 kg/ha
→ K: >280 kg/ha

🔴 PROBLEMATIC LEVELS:
→ pH <5.5 = Acidic (add lime)
→ pH >8.5 = Saline/Alkaline (add gypsum)
→ EC >1.5 = Saline (leach with water)
→ Organic Carbon <0.5% = Very poor (add FYM)

📍 WHERE TO TEST FREE:
→ Soil Health Card Scheme: soilhealth.dac.gov.in
→ Nearest KVK office
→ State Agriculture University labs
→ IARI regional stations

💧 DRIP IRRIGATION — Complete Setup Guide:

🔧 COMPONENTS:
1. Filter unit (Sand + Disc/Screen filter)
2. Fertilizer tank (Venturi / Bypass)
3. Main line (75–110mm HDPE pipe)
4. Sub-main (50–75mm pipe)
5. Lateral pipe (12–16mm inline emitter)
6. Emitters/drippers (2 LPH or 4 LPH)
7. Pressure gauge & flow meter

📐 LAYOUT FOR ONION:
→ Lateral spacing: 1.5 m (for 2 rows between)
→ Emitter spacing: 30–45 cm
→ Emitter discharge: 2 LPH
→ Operating pressure: 1.5–2 kg/cm²

⏰ IRRIGATION SCHEDULING:
→ Run time = (Water requirement per day × Area) / (Emitters × discharge)
→ Onion: 40–50 min/day in summer
→ Tomato: 50–60 min/day
→ Grape: 2–3 hours/day (summer)

💊 FERTIGATION SCHEDULE:
→ 80% of fertilizer through drip (rest basal)
→ Weekly schedule: N 2 days + P 1 day + K 2 days
→ Don't mix P and Ca together (precipitates!)

🔧 MAINTENANCE:
→ Flush laterals weekly
→ Clean filters every 3 days
→ Check pressure at farthest dripper monthly

💧 WATER MANAGEMENT — Critical Crop Stages:

🚨 WATER STRESS AT THESE STAGES = BIG YIELD LOSS!

🌾 WHEAT:
→ Crown Root Initiation (21 days) — most critical!
→ Jointing (60 days)
→ Grain filling (90 days)
→ Total: 400–450 mm water

🧅 ONION:
→ Bulb formation (60–90 days) — very critical!
→ Never waterlog — causes rot
→ Total: 450–500 mm water

🍅 TOMATO:
→ Flowering & fruit set — critical
→ Fruit development
→ Irregular irrigation = cracking + BER
→ Total: 500–600 mm water

🌿 COTTON:
→ Flowering (60 days)
→ Boll development (90–120 days)
→ Total: 600–700 mm water

💡 AWD (Alternate Wetting & Drying) for Rice:
→ Flood → Let dry until 15cm below → Refill
→ Saves 30% water without yield loss
→ Reduces methane emissions!

📱 Soil moisture sensors:
→ Tensiometer (cheapest, ₹800–1,500)
→ Soil moisture meter (digital, ₹2,000–5,000)
→ Use them to optimize irrigation timing!

💧 WATER CONSERVATION TIPS FOR FARMERS:

🌿 MULCHING (Most effective!):
→ Spread dry straw/leaves 2–3 inch thick
→ Reduces water evaporation by 40–60%
→ Black polythene mulch: Most effective
→ Silver mulch: Repels aphids & whitefly too!
→ Cost: ₹3,000–5,000/acre (with subsidy available)

🌱 CONTOUR FARMING (Slopes):
→ Farm along contours, not downhill
→ Prevents runoff, increases water absorption
→ Bunds at 15–20m intervals

→ Ferment 48 hours, stir clockwise
→ Apply: 200L/acre/month
→ Benefits: 10 crore microbes/ml!

2️⃣ BEEJAMRIT (Seed Treatment):
→ 5L water + 50g cow dung + 50ml cow urine
→ 50g lime + 1 handful soil
→ Soak seeds 8–10 hours OR coat & dry
→ Protects seeds from soil-borne diseases

3️⃣ MULCHING:
→ Cover soil with dry straw/leaves (3–4 inch)
→ Keeps soil moist & cool
→ Suppresses weeds
→ Feeds soil microorganisms

4️⃣ WAAPHASA (Soil Aeration):
→ Less water, more air in soil
→ Alternate wetting → drying
→ Saves 50% irrigation water

🏆 RESULTS: 30–50% less cost, same or better yield!

🌿 VERMICOMPOST — Make at Home:

🪱 EARTHWORM COMPOSTING GUIDE:

📦 SETUP:
→ Size: 3m × 1m × 0.6m brick/cemented bed
→ OR: Old cement rings / wooden boxes
→ Shade required (no direct sun)
→ Keep moist (60–70% moisture)

🌱 MATERIALS:
→ Agricultural waste: Crop residue, straw, leaves
→ Kitchen waste: Vegetable peels, fruit waste
→ Animal dung: Cow, buffalo, goat dung
→ Avoid: Meat, oily food, citrus waste, plastic

🪱 EARTHWORMS TO USE:
→ Eisenia fetida (Red wriggler) — best!
→ Lumbricus rubellus
→ Buy: ₹200–400/kg from KVK or online

⏰ PROCESS:
→ Fill bed with moistened organic waste
→ Add earthworms on top
→ Cover with gunny bag
→ Water every 2–3 days
→ Ready in: 45–60 days
→ Color: Dark brown, earthy smell, crumbly

💰 ECONOMICS:
→ Input cost: ₹2–3/kg
→ Selling price: ₹6–12/kg
→ 1 tonne worms produce 1 tonne compost/month!
→ Sell liquid (leachate) too: ₹50/L as foliar spray

📈 INCOME: ₹10,000–20,000/month from 3×1m bed!

🏪 POST-HARVEST STORAGE GUIDE:

🧅 ONION STORAGE:
→ Moisture: 12–14% (cure in sun 7–10 days)
→ Temp: Ambient (25–30°C)
→ Humidity: 65–70%
→ Structure: Ventilated bamboo/wire-mesh chhajali
→ Shelf life: 6–12 months (Rabi, less for Kharif)
→ Expected losses: 10–15%

🥔 POTATO:
→ Cold storage: 2–4°C, 90–95% RH
→ Duration: 6–9 months in cold storage
→ Without cold storage: 2–3 months max
→ Treatment: CIPC (sprout inhibitor) @ 30 ppm
→ Cost: ₹150–250/quintal/season

🌾 WHEAT/GRAIN:
→ Dry to <12% moisture (CRITICAL!)
→ Use PUSA moisture-free bins
→ Or hermetic bags (Cocoon, ZeroFly bags)
→ Insect treatment: Malathion 5% dust 50g/100 kg
→ Aluminum Phosphide (Celphos): 1 tablet/tonne
→ Duration: 12–24 months

🌽 MAIZE:
→ Moisture: <13.5% before storing
→ Cob maize: Hang in well-ventilated shed
→ Grain maize: HDPE bin with fumigation
→ Check for aflatoxin in hot/humid conditions!

🏪 GRAIN STORAGE — Preventing Losses:

💡 UP TO 20% LOSSES CAN HAPPEN IN STORAGE! Prevent them:

🔍 COMMON PESTS IN STORED GRAIN:
• Weevil (Sitophilus): Wheat, Rice, Maize
• Flour Beetle (Tribolium): Flour products
• Pulse Beetle (Callosobruchus): Dal, Gram
• Khapra Beetle: Most destructive!

🛡️ STORAGE TREATMENTS:

1️⃣ PHYSICAL:
→ Clean & dry grain before storage (<12% moisture)
→ Clean store room (lime-wash walls & ceiling)
→ Use airtight storage (hermetic bags)
→ Regular inspection every 15 days

2️⃣ CHEMICAL:
→ Malathion 50 EC: Surface spray 5 ml/sq.m
→ DDVP (Vapona): Vapors for flying insects
→ Aluminum Phosphide (Celphos) tablet:
   • 1 tablet per tonne grain
   • Seal storage 5–7 days
   • HIGHLY TOXIC — use with extreme caution!
   • Licensed dealer only

3️⃣ NATURAL:
→ Neem leaves mixing: 2 kg leaves/100 kg grain (dal)
→ Ash mixing: Reduce moisture, repel insects
→ Turmeric powder: Pulse beetle control

4️⃣ MODERN:
→ Hermetic bags (ZeroFly, Super Bag): No chemical needed
→ PUSA metal bins: 1–2 tonne capacity
→ CO2 treatment: Available at some state stores

☁️ WEATHER & FARMING — Seasonal Guide:

🌧️ KHARIF SEASON (Jun–Sep) — Monsoon Crops:
→ Key crops: Rice, Soybean, Cotton, Maize
→ Sow 2–3 weeks after monsoon onset
→ Ensure proper drainage (avoid waterlogging)
→ Watch for: Blast, Leaf diseases in humid weather
→ Pest alert: Aphids, Whitefly increase in cloudy days

❄️ RABI SEASON (Oct–Mar) — Winter Crops:
→ Key crops: Wheat, Onion, Mustard, Gram
→ Frost risk in Dec–Jan (cover young plants)
→ Fog: Increases Powdery Mildew, Purple Blotch risk
→ Spray preventive fungicide before forecasted fog
→ Irrigation: Reduce on foggy days

☀️ ZAID SEASON (Mar–Jun) — Summer Crops:
→ Key crops: Watermelon, Cucumber, Pumpkin, Moong
→ High evaporation = more irrigation needed
→ Shade nets: 40–50% for seedlings
→ High temps: Use mulching to conserve moisture

📱 WEATHER APPS FOR FARMERS:
→ Meghdoot App (IMD): Block-level 5-day forecast
→ Damini App: Lightning alerts
→ mKisan: SMS weather advisory
→ Mausam App: IMD official app

☁️ CLIMATE RISK MANAGEMENT FOR FARMERS:

⚠️ EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS & RESPONSE:

🌊 FLOOD/HEAVY RAIN:
→ Pre-season: Prepare drainage channels
→ During: Don't spray chemicals
→ Post-flood:
  • Drain water within 48 hours
  • Spray Urea 2% (foliar) after drainage
  • Watch for bacterial/fungal diseases
  • Apply Mancozeb preventively
  • File insurance claim within 72 hours!

🏜️ DROUGHT/LOW RAINFALL:
→ Use drought-tolerant varieties
→ Mulching: Reduces water need by 40%
→ Drip irrigation: Critical investment
→ Contingency crops: Switch to Bajra, Sesame
→ Crop insurance PMFBY covers drought!

❄️ FROST/COLD WAVE:
→ Sprinkle irrigation before frost (latent heat protection)
→ Smoke screens (old method)
→ Plastic mulch on beds
→ Apply KNO3 (Potassium Nitrate) spray @ 1%

🌪️ HAILSTORM:
→ Anti-hail nets (polyhouse): 35% subsidy available
→ After hailstorm:
  • Spray Copper Oxychloride 3g/L
  • Spray Urea 2% (recovery spray)
  • File insurance claim with photos

☁️ WEATHER-BASED CROP ADVISORY:

🌡️ TEMPERATURE EFFECTS ON CROPS:

🔥 HIGH TEMPERATURE (>35°C effects):
• Wheat: Grain shriveling, yield reduction
• Tomato: Flower drop, fruit cracking
• Grape: Sunburn on berries, color loss
• Onion: Bulb deformation, bolting

💊 REMEDIES FOR HEAT STRESS:
→ Kaolin clay spray 5% (reflects heat)
→ Potassium Nitrate 13:0:45 spray 1%
→ Micronutrient spray (Zinc + Boron)
→ Shade net (35% shade) for vegetables
→ Light irrigation at evening

🥶 LOW TEMPERATURE (<10°C effects):
• Banana, Papaya: Chilling injury
• Groundnut: Stunted growth
• Mango, Citrus: Flower drop

💊 REMEDIES FOR COLD STRESS:
→ KNO3 spray 1% before frost
→ Thiourea spray 0.05% (anti-stress)
→ Cover with polythene
→ Light overhead irrigation

🍇 GRAPE FARMING — Complete Guide (Nashik Region):

📍 Nashik = India's Grape Capital!

🌡️ Climate: 15–40°C
🌱 Popular varieties:
• Thompson Seedless (export king)
• Sharad Seedless (domestic)
• Flame Seedless (red, premium)
• Manik Chaman (local market)
• 2A Clone (late maturing)

🏗️ TRAINING SYSTEMS:
• Bower (Pandal) system: Most common in Nashik
  → Cost: ₹1.5–2 lakh/acre
  → Better air circulation, less disease
• Telephone/Tatura trellis: Commercial
• Head training (Desi): Old method

💊 FERTILIZER PER VINE/YEAR:
→ N: 600g | P: 200g | K: 600g
→ Apply in 4 splits: Apr, Jun, Aug, Oct
→ Calcium Nitrate: 10 g/L (10–12 sprays)
→ Boron: 2 g/L at berry initiation
→ Magnesium Sulphate: 10 g/L foliar

🌊 IRRIGATION: Drip preferred
→ Summer: 25–30 L/vine/day
→ Winter: 10–15 L/vine/day

📈 YIELD: 20–30 tonnes/ha (commercial)
💰 Domestic: ₹30–80/kg | Export: ₹60–120/kg

🍇 GRAPE — Disease & Pest Management:

🦠 TOP DISEASES:

1. DOWNY MILDEW (Plasmopara viticola) 🚨 #1 Problem!
→ Yellow spots on upper leaf, white downy on underside
→ Favors cool, humid weather
→ SPRAY SCHEDULE:
  • Metalaxyl + Mancozeb @ 2.5 g/L (preventive)
  • Dimethomorph 50 WP @ 1 g/L (curative)
  • Fosetyl Aluminium @ 2.5 g/L
→ Spray every 7 days in humid weather

2. POWDERY MILDEW (Uncinula necator)
→ White powdery coating on all parts
→ Favors dry weather with moderate humidity
→ Treatment: Hexaconazole @ 0.1%
→ Or: Wettable Sulphur @ 3 g/L
→ Spray every 10 days

3. ANTHRACNOSE
→ Dark circular spots on leaves/berries/shoots
→ Mancozeb @ 2.5 g/L

4. BOTRYTIS BUNCH ROT
→ Grey mold on bunches
→ Iprodione 50 WP @ 1.5 g/L

🪲 PESTS:
• THRIPS: Spinosad 45 SC @ 0.3 ml/L
• MEALYBUG: Chlorpyriphos + Cypermethrin
• LEAF ROLLER: Chlorantraniliprole @ 0.3 ml/L
• SPIDER MITE: Abamectin 1.8 EC @ 0.5 ml/L

💡 TIMING: Most sprays needed at bud burst stage!

🌿 SUGARCANE — Ratoon Management:

♻️ RATOON CROP = 60% COST SAVING!

After harvest, don't destroy the roots — take ratoon!

📋 STEPS FOR GOOD RATOON:

1️⃣ Immediately after harvest:
→ Apply Urea @ 25 kg/acre + water
→ Earthing up within 15 days
→ Gap filling (replant dried stools)

2️⃣ NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT:
→ 80% of plant crop nutrition is sufficient
→ FYM/Vermicompost: 5–8 t/ha (top dress)
→ N application: Split in 3 doses

3️⃣ DISEASE MANAGEMENT:
→ Red rot resistant varieties for ratoon
→ Ratoon stunting: Use healthy sets only in next planting
→ Apply Trichoderma around stools

4️⃣ WEED CONTROL:
→ Atrazine 50 WP @ 2 kg/ha (pre-emergence)
→ 2 hand weedings: 30 & 60 days
→ Trash mulching: Best weed control!

⚠️ WHEN TO AVOID RATOON:
→ If Wilt/Smut/Red Rot attacked >10% plants
→ If soil has nematode problem
→ After 2nd ratoon: Soil rejuvenation needed

💰 RATOON ECONOMICS:
→ Input cost: 40% less vs plant crop
→ Usually same or slightly lower yield
→ Profitable if managed well!
